title: Productize Autoplan
use_when: Use Productize Autoplan when the user wants Productize to inspect the current plan, build stage, release, or production decision and automatically select the relevant gates in the right order.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Productize Autoplan when the user has already named a specific gate or needs a tactical routed skill artifact. Run the named gate or routed skill directly unless routing across multiple gates is the actual task.
output_artifact: Autoplan gate matrix with current stage, gate set, review order, needed artifacts, and exit criteria
framework_fit: Productize Autoplan fits cross-gate coordination when the user does not want to manually choose between thesis, product, design, engineering, QA, release, docs, DX, and comms gates. It is a meta-runner for gate selection, not a product artifact generator.
failure_modes: Runs every gate by default instead of selecting only the gates made relevant by the current playbook stage, evidence, and blocked decision.
Produces a generic checklist without sequencing gates, identifying stale artifacts, or stating what each gate needs to inspect.
Uses autoplan as a substitute for the actual 0-1, operate, or grow playbook instead of returning gate calls that unblock the active loop.

examples.prompt: Use $autoplan to inspect this implementation plan and tell me which Productize gates should run now, in what order, and what each gate needs.
examples.expected_artifact: Autoplan gate matrix with current stage, gate set, review order, needed artifacts, and exit criteria
